meta.document: Open Accreditation System entity model AI context
notes: Logical model only; not a finalized physical schema.
Keep the core domain accreditor-agnostic across AACSB, ABET, HLC, and future frameworks.
Person is the canonical human concept; User is the authenticated platform identity projection of a person.
EvidenceReference uses a governed polymorphic target pattern with explicit allowed target types and owning-context validation responsibilities.
Assessment scope is expressed through canonical program/course/section/term/outcome references, not accreditor-specific templates.
AI outputs are advisory and require human review.

entityRules.NarrativeSection: Mutable while drafting; preserve submitted/finalized versions through snapshot or explicit version capture.
entityRules.AssessmentPlan: Supersede/version after approval or after governed results depend on it.
entityRules.AssessmentMeasure: Supersede/version after results exist or design changes materially.
entityRules.DecisionRecord: Append-only after issuance; corrections create linked successor records.
entityRules.EvidenceRequirement: Versioned with FrameworkVersion; do not overwrite published requirements in place.
entityRules.FacultyDeployment: Effective-dated and supersedable for historical sufficiency analysis.
entityRules.FacultyQualification: Supersedable determination root; preserve the sequence of qualification states.
entityRules.QualificationBasis: Append-only or explicit successor-basis pattern; never silently rewrite rationale.
entityRules.ReviewTeamMembership: Effective-dated; preserve roster history and role/conflict changes.
entityRules.CycleMilestone: Effective-dated or supersedable; retain replanning history.

validationResponsibility.evidence-management: validate allow-listed targetEntityType and local reference invariants
validationResponsibility.targetOwningContext: validate target existence and state through published application interfaces

evidenceReferenceContract.extensionRule: Additive only: update docs, AI context, and target-owning contract together; consumers must tolerate newer unsupported target types.

invalidPatterns: AcademicTerm alone as the only assessment subject in normal cases
Program plus unrelated CourseSection without explicit curriculum mapping or scope rationale
Measure/result scope introducing targets not present or implied in the plan

accreditationFrameworksEpic1: FrameworkVersion publication requires at least one Standard and at least one CriterionElement under each Criterion.
FrameworkVersion structural children (Standard, Criterion, CriterionElement, EvidenceRequirement) are mutable only while status=draft.
EvidenceRequirement must target criterionId or criterionElementId in the same FrameworkVersion; if both are set, they must align.
AccreditationCycle must reference a published FrameworkVersion.
AccreditationScope must include at least one Program or OrganizationUnit reference.
CycleMilestone, ReviewEvent, and AccreditationScope dates must stay within AccreditationCycle start/end dates.
DecisionRecord is append-only after issuance; corrections are represented by successor records that supersede prior decisions.
evidenceManagementEpic2Phase1: EvidenceItem is a first-class aggregate root for governed accreditation evidence metadata.
EvidenceItem.evidenceType must be one of: document, metric, narrative, dataset, assessment-artifact.
EvidenceItem.sourceType must be one of: manual, upload, integration.
EvidenceItem.status must be one of: draft, active, superseded, incomplete, archived.
EvidenceItem lifecycle status is evidence-governance state and must not duplicate workflow approval state.
EvidenceItem.create starts with status=draft, isComplete=false, and no supersededByEvidenceItemId.
EvidenceItem.evidenceLineageId identifies evidence lineage and defaults to the initial EvidenceItem.id for version 1.
EvidenceItem.versionNumber starts at 1 and versionNumber>1 requires supersedesEvidenceItemId.
EvidenceItem must not embed artifact storage fields; storage metadata belongs to EvidenceArtifact child records.
EvidenceArtifact.evidenceItemId must match the owning EvidenceItem.id.
EvidenceItem lifecycle transitions are explicit: draft->(incomplete|active|archived), incomplete->(draft|archived), active->(incomplete|superseded|archived), superseded/archived terminal.
EvidenceItem.currentArtifact resolves to the most recent available artifact or null when none are available.
EvidenceItem.status=active requires non-empty description, at least one of reportingPeriodId/reviewCycleId, and isComplete=true.
EvidenceItem.status=active requires at least one available EvidenceArtifact when sourceType=upload or evidenceType=document/dataset/assessment-artifact.
EvidenceItem.status=active may omit artifacts for evidenceType=metric/narrative when sourceType=manual/integration.
EvidenceItem.status=incomplete requires isComplete=false.
EvidenceItem.status=superseded requires supersededByEvidenceItemId.
Artifact registration is allowed only while EvidenceItem.status is draft or incomplete.
EvidenceItem.status=superseded and EvidenceItem.status=archived are terminal for lifecycle transitions in this phase.
Superseding EvidenceItem requires successor existence and same institution.

Application-layer supersession requires lineage-consistent successor identity (same evidenceLineageId, successor.supersedesEvidenceItemId = predecessor.id, and successor.versionNumber = predecessor.versionNumber + 1).
EvidenceArtifact persistence is append-only in this phase; existing artifact metadata cannot be changed or removed in-place.
EvidenceReference target associations are append-only in this phase; existing reference metadata cannot be changed or removed in-place.
Repository save boundaries rehydrate/validate EvidenceItem aggregate snapshots before persistence to prevent in-memory invariant bypass.
EvidenceReference target admissibility is validated centrally in evidence-management and target existence/admissibility checks are delegated through target-owning application interfaces.
EvidenceReference linkage annotations are normalized and target-governed; narrative-section targets require anchorPath.

Evidence retrieval supports explicit target queries (criterion, criterion-element, learning-outcome, narrative-section), lineage version-state filters (current/historical/all), linkage-context projection, cycle/reporting anchor filtering, and within-cycle vs cross-cycle supersession-readiness summaries.
workflowApprovalsPhase3: ReviewCycle is a first-class aggregate root for workflow-governed review cycle orchestration.
ReviewCycle.status must be one of: not-started, active, completed, archived.
ReviewCycle.startDate must be strictly earlier than endDate.
Only one active ReviewCycle is allowed for the same institution+scope key (canonicalized programIds and organizationUnitIds).
ReviewWorkflow is a first-class aggregate root bound to a ReviewCycle and target context (for example report section or evidence grouping).
ReviewWorkflow.state must be one of: draft, in-review, revision-required, approved, submitted.
ReviewWorkflow state transitions are explicit and restricted to draft->in-review, in-review->revision-required|approved, revision-required->draft, approved->submitted.
Workflow role policy is enforced for faculty, reviewer, and admin transitions.
Workflow transition history is append-only and cannot be mutated in-place.
Workflow evidence integration is reference-based (evidenceItemIds/evidenceCollectionId) and does not embed workflow state in EvidenceItem.
Approval/submission transitions evaluate referenced evidence completeness, activity status, and usability.
crossContextReferenceRules: accreditation-frameworks validates local shape and ownership invariants for framework and cycle aggregates.
organization-registry validates institution and organization-unit existence via application ports.
curriculum-mapping validates program existence via application ports.
evidence-management, workflow-approvals, and assessment-improvement may reference accreditation IDs but do not mutate accreditation-frameworks aggregates.
